en.extract:
Bertram's weaver (Ploceus bertrandi), sometimes called Bertrand's weaver, is a species of bird in the family Ploceidae.
It is native to the Eastern Arc forests and mountains of Malawi.

It is named after Bertram Lutley Sclater, the son of ornithologist Philip Lutley Sclater, who was a police commissioner in Malawi when Alexander Whyte discovered this species.


References


External links

'''Bertram's weaver''' ('''''Ploceus bertrandi'''''), sometimes called '''Bertrand's weaver''', is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Ploceidae]].

It is native to the [[Eastern Arc forests]] and mountains of Malawi.
[[File:Ploceus bertrandi - Tom Field - 472861183.jpeg|left|thumb|Live bird in [[Tanzania]]]]
It is named after Bertram Lutley Sclater, the son of ornithologist [[Philip Lutley Sclater]], who was a police commissioner in Malawi when Alexander Whyte discovered this species.<ref>{{cite book |last1=Boelens |first1=Bo |last2=Watkins |first2=Michael |title=Whose Bird?: Common Bird Names and the People They Commemorate |year=2003 |publisher=Yale University Press|isbn=0-300-10359-X}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal|journal=The Ibis |series=8th series |volume= V (1)|issue= XVII|pages=1–29 |last= Shelley|first= George Ernest|title=I. List of Birds collected by Mr. Alexander Whyte, F.Z.S., in Nyassaland}}</ref>
